items.title: What was the World Cup referee given to drink during the USA vs Australia game and why does it work?

items.summary: 43-year-old led French side to Conference League semis Burnley pushing to bring in Craig Bellamy as head coach Gary O’Neil is poised to become Ipswich’s new head coach, with the 43-year-old expected to return to England from Ligue 1 Strasbourg. items.title: Ex-West Ham star Michail Antonio to face trial after having driving conviction quashed
items.summary: EX-West Ham ace Michail Antonio is to face trial after having a driving ­conviction quashed. The striker’s Lamborghini Urus was clocked at 50mph in a 40mph zone in Birmingham in March 2025. items.title: Leinster retain URC title after battering Bulls
items.summary: Leinster demolished the Bulls 36-7 to retain their United Rugby Championship title but the victory could have repercussions for Ireland after Caelan Doris and Tommy O'Brien sustained injuries.

items.title: New Zealand edge Ireland in thriller to keep World Cup hopes alive
items.summary: Defending champions New Zealand beat Ireland by four runs to keep their ICC Women's T20 World Cup hopes alive in a thriller that went down to the final ball of the match.

items.title: Brighton make improved offer for Spurs' Vuskovic
items.summary: Brighton have made an improved offer to Tottenham Hotspur for centre-back Luka Vuskovic.

USA secure World Cup knockout spot with win over Australia
items.summary: Co-hosts USA secured their place in the World Cup knockout stages with a 2-0 victory over Australia in Group D.

items.title: USA close in on last 32 with win over Australia
items.summary: The United States take a huge step towards winning their World Cup group beating Australia 2-0 with a dominant, high-energy performance in front of a boisterous sellout crowd in Seattle.

items.title: Neymar embroiled in World Cup row with Brazil PRESIDENT who accuses him of ‘working from home’
items.summary: BRAZIL’S President has accused injured superstar Neymar of “working from home” at the World Cup. items.title: No Pulisic? No problem: Freeman, Adams lead the wa...
items.summary: Strong performances from Tyler Adams and Malik Tillman earned high ratings, but it was Alex Freeman who stood out.

items.title: Portugal's Dias: Ronaldo criticism 'nothing new'
items.summary: Portugal defender Rúben Dias on Friday branded criticism of teammate Cristiano Ronaldo "insignificant" and claimed it has not affected the team's focus.

items.title: Arsenal start new PL season against Coventry
items.summary: Arsenal will begin their Premier League title defence against newly-promoted Coventry City, while Liverpool travel to face Newcastle in the pick of the opening-day fixtures.

items.title: Pulisic sits but U.S. finds a way vs. Australia to...
items.summary: The USMNT was without Christian Pulisic on Friday, but it didn't matter: it found a way to beat Australia for its second win of this World Cup.

items.title: U.S. sees off Australia, advances to WC knockouts
items.summary: The U.S. shook off the absence of Christian Pulisic to beat Australia 2-0 in Seattle on Friday and clinch a spot in the World Cup knockout rounds.

items.title: Iran to file complaint over WC travel restrictions...
items.summary: Iran's soccer federation said it will lodge a complaint with FIFA after its request to travel to Los Angeles two days before Sunday's World Cup match against Belgium was denied.

items.title: Potter: Sweden a 'threat' with Gyökeres, Isak duo
items.summary: Graham Potter has warned Sweden's World Cup opponents that strike-duo Alexander Isak and Viktor Gyökeres will get better as the tournament progresses.

items.title: Korea GK gaffe helps Mexico clinch 1st in Group A
items.summary: Mexico took advantage of a defensive blunder by South Korea to win 1-0 on Thursday and become the first team to advance to the knockout stage of the FIFA World Cup.
